evals.name: agglutinative-finnish-paradigm-augmentation
evals.prompt: I'm fine-tuning Llama on Finnish but only have ~20K Finnish examples. Can morphology help?
evals.expected_output: Should: (1) classify Finnish as tier=hi, agglutinative-Uralic; (2) note UniMorph coverage is DEEP for Finnish; (3) recommend paradigm-completion augmentation (UniMorph paradigms × 10-30 inflected forms per lemma); (4) tag synthetic with <morph_aug>; (5) expected gain ~1-3 BLEU; (6) HFST-fin (Omorfi) is the FST analyzer if needed; (7) hand off to linguistic-tokenize for fertility re-audit.

evals.name: templatic-arabic-trap
evals.prompt: I'm trying to use Morfessor to segment Arabic morphemes for my LLM tokenizer. Should I?
evals.expected_output: Should: (1) NO — Morfessor is concatenative; Arabic is templatic non-concatenative; (2) explain root-pattern (k-t-b root with various patterns producing kataba, kitab, maktab, etc.); (3) recommend Farasa or MADAMIRA instead (root-aware); (4) note segmentation BEFORE BPE; vocab should include root + pattern markers; (5) generalize to Hebrew (YAP) and Amharic (HornMorpho).

evals.name: polysynthetic-inuktitut
evals.prompt: How do I tokenize Inuktitut for my MT model? Each word seems insanely long.
evals.expected_output: Should: (1) tier=extreme polysynthetic; (2) explain words can be 8-20+ morphemes; (3) MANDATORY morpheme segmentation BEFORE BPE; (4) HFST-iku (Inuit-FST) or HFST-kal for Greenlandic — use if available; (5) SIGMORPHON 2023 segmenter as fallback; (6) tokenizer alone insufficient — vocab extension critical (route to linguistic-tokenize); (7) flag community partnership for community-controlled data.
